svg.text: Why we chose Time-of-Flight over Ultrasonic — a full engineering breakdown
How fast the sensor reports a value
WRO track surface is glossy white — this matters

g.text: laser reflects off mat → false readings

svg.text: How each sensor reads narrow obstacle pillars

g.text: reads it as wall — handled in software
precise enough for continuous PD control

svg.text: Software overhead to clean up readings
Sensitivity to mounting height above ground
OUR DECISION — WHY WE CHOSE VL53L0X ToF ×3
The VL53L0X wins where it counts most for WRO:

g.text: Accuracy of ±1mm enables precise wall-angle calculation and continuous PD steering — impossible with ±3cm ultrasonic
50Hz refresh vs 20Hz means 2.5× faster obstacle reaction — critical at competition speeds
Tradeoff accepted: glossy mat interference and critical mounting requirements — solved with careful height calibration and
XSHUT-pin I2C initialization. The precision gain outweighs the setup complexity.

svg.text: SUNBIRD NOMADS · WRO 2026 FUTURE ENGINEERS · PALESTINE
